Metadata.title: BitRouter — Agent-native LLM Router
Metadata.description: Agent-native LLM router that optimizes your agent with every run. Zero harness changes — every model call reliable, traceable, secure, and cost-effective. Open-sourced, Cloud opt-in.

Hero.headline: Agent-native LLM router that optimizes your agent with every run.
Hero.headlineRich: Agent-native LLM router that optimizes your agent with <accent>every run</accent>.
Hero.subhead: Zero harness changes — every model call reliable, traceable, secure, and cost-effective. Open-sourced, Cloud opt-in.

UseCases.label: USE CASES
UseCases.dek: Coding agents run into these first. Every autonomous agent runs into them eventually.

longRuns.name: agent · run_8x2k
longRuns.tagline: Your coding agent is 140 files into a refactor when a provider rate-limits it, and the run dies. Every long agent loop breaks the same way — one blip, back to step zero.

points.title: Overnight jobs that finish
points.description: Failover keeps long autonomous sessions alive when a single provider blips or throttles.

points.title: No babysitting rate limits
points.description: A 429 or 503 reroutes to another provider instead of ending the run.

points.title: The agent never sees the failure
points.description: Retries and reroutes happen inside the router — the agent loop just keeps going.

observability.name: billing · october
observability.tagline: Your coding agent ran all week and the bill is a single number. Which task, which step, which model burned it? Every agent fleet is the same black box.

points.title: Cost attributed per run
points.description: See what a single run cost, rolled up from every call inside it.

points.title: Every hop, every model, traced
points.description: The full call chain — each agent, model, and step — instead of one opaque total.

points.title: No more black-box bills
points.description: Find the expensive step in a run instead of guessing from a monthly invoice.

security.name: issue #4127
security.tagline: A coding agent reads an issue, a README, a dependency — and any one can smuggle in an instruction you never wrote. Any agent reading the open web or a tool result faces the same, with no human watching.

points.title: Injection caught at the router
points.description: Prompt-injection detection runs on every call, before the agent acts on it.

points.title: Secrets filtered from output
points.description: Output filtering strips keys and secrets so an agent can't leak them downstream.

points.title: One policy for every agent
points.description: Set guardrails once at the router — every agent on it inherits them.

efficiency.name: receipt · run_8x2k
efficiency.tagline: A coding agent fires hundreds of calls a run, most of them trivial. Send every one to your strongest model and the bill is mostly waste — the same for any agent that loops.

points.title: Right model per step
points.description: Cheap models for boilerplate, your strongest for the hard diffs — chosen automatically.

points.title: Up to −90% per run
points.description: Routing by task complexity cuts the bill against an always-top-model baseline.

points.title: Savings that compound
points.description: Every pipeline gets the same routing — the gain repeats across every run.

Models.metaDescription: One API for the most performant, reliable models for LLM agents — across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, and more.
Models.headlineTrail: The most performant, reliable models for LLM agents.
Models.subtitle: Every model routable through BitRouter — filter by context window or price band, then copy the model ID into your client. Want to know which provider actually serves each model? <link>See the provider registry</link>.
Models.emptyBody: Try removing a filter or clearing the search.

ModelDetail.quickstartBody: Drop the model ID into either endpoint — BitRouter speaks the OpenAI and Anthropic wire formats natively.
ModelDetail.metaDescription: Route {id} through BitRouter. {input} input, {output} output, {context} context.

Providers.metaDescription: The permissionless provider registry behind BitRouter. Browse every provider currently routing inference through the network, or PR your own.
Providers.heroHeadlineTrail: Every provider here is one YAML in a public repo — anyone can PR a new one.
Providers.heroBody: These are the providers currently listed in <link>bitrouter/provider-registry</link>. There’s no gatekeeper, no application, no review queue — the proxy reads the registry, and BitRouter routes to whichever listed provider serves a given model fastest and cheapest.
Providers.howABody: Add providers/yours.yaml — name, base URL, the models you serve, and your prices.
Providers.howBBody: The schema check runs in CI. Once green, BitRouter picks up your listing on the next refresh.
Providers.howCBody: When an agent calls a model you serve, BitRouter weighs price, latency, and your declared weight to decide.
Providers.emptyBody: The bitrouter/provider-registry repository returned no listings. Try again in a moment, or browse the registry directly on GitHub.
Providers.noMatchBody: Try a different search or clear the filter.
Providers.contributeHeadline: Serve your capacity. Earn on every routed call.
Providers.contributeBody: Whether you’re a frontier lab, a regional GPU host, or a hobbyist with a homelab — one PR makes you addressable from every BitRouter client on the network.

ProviderDetail.body: Listed in the BitRouter provider registry — rendered straight from the source YAML so what you see here is exactly what the proxy reads when it routes.
ProviderDetail.noRateLimits: No rate limits declared in the YAML.
ProviderDetail.modelListingsBody: The exact set of models {name} serves through BitRouter, with prices straight from the registry. Click any row to view the model in the catalog.
ProviderDetail.noModels: No models listed in this provider’s YAML.
ProviderDetail.cheapestOutputCaption: Cheapest output: {value} / 1M tokens. Prices in USD per million tokens.
ProviderDetail.metaDescription: {name} routes {count, plural, =0 {no models} =1 {one model} other {# models}} through BitRouter. Live from the open provider registry.

FAQ.label: FAQ
FAQ.supportingCopy: Common questions about pricing, routing, and data handling. If yours isn’t here, <docs>check the docs</docs> or <call>talk to us</call> — we usually reply within a day.
FAQ.title: Questions before you ship.
FAQ.subtitle: Common questions about pricing, routing, and data handling.

questions.q: How is BitRouter different from OpenRouter?
questions.a: OpenRouter is a closed-source hosted gateway — no self-host option, no agent-native primitives, no permissionless registry. BitRouter is Apache 2.0: fork the binary and run it anywhere, or use the hosted edge if you don’t want to operate it. The provider registry is open — anyone can publish a provider via PR, no review queue.

questions.q: How is BitRouter different from LiteLLM?
questions.a: LiteLLM is an open-source Python library you embed in your app. BitRouter is a standalone binary with auth, billing, observability, guardrails, and an MCP/ACP/Skills gateway built in. Drop it in front of any runtime — Claude Code, Cursor, your own agent — without wiring it into every service.

questions.q: How does BitRouter guarantee service reliability?
questions.a: Every routing alias retries with exponential backoff and fails over cross-provider on 429s or upstream errors — a single provider outage doesn’t kill an agent run. Per-provider health and incident history are visible in the console. Failed requests aren’t billed.

questions.q: How is the markup fee calculated?
questions.a: Added on top of the upstream provider’s published rate — 2% with USDC/USDT over x402/MPP, 5% with card (Stripe’s 3% fee absorbed). No token markup, no minimums, no monthly fee. Self-host has no platform fee at all.

questions.q: How do I register as a provider on BitRouter?
questions.a: Open a PR against `bitrouter/provider-registry` with a YAML listing your name, base URL, the models you serve, prices, and routing weight. CI runs schema checks; once green, BitRouter picks up your listing on the next refresh and starts routing agent traffic. No application, no gatekeeper.

Pull the Apache 2.0 binary from `github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter` — single binary, no daemon, no GUI, no infrastructure dependencies. Drops into any container or CI step. You get the same routing, guardrails, MCP/ACP/Skills gateway, and observability as the hosted edge — without the platform fee.

Features.label: BUILT FOR AGENTS
Features.dek: Four mechanisms, built into the router — not bolted on per agent.

reliability.body: Reroutes across providers mid-run, transparently — your agent never sees the failed call.

observability.body: Full call-chain visibility: every agent, every model, every step, with cost attributed per run.

security.body: Prompt-injection detection, output filtering, and rate limits — enforced at the router, once, for every agent.

efficiency.body: Matches each call to the right model by task complexity — savings that compound across every run.

CTA.dek: One API key, every model. No monthly fees, no lock-in. Self-host or use the hosted edge.
CTA.pricing: 2% on usage · no platform fee under $10/mo

LandingFooter.tagline: Open-source gateway for agent runtimes. Route to any model, any provider — one API.
